title: Prompt Lifecycle, Monitoring, and Production Capstone · Eval Recall
summary: Ship and maintain a cross-provider prompt system with regression evidence. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a production prompt portfolio and release gate after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Store owners, versions, model assumptions, templates, and evaluation links in a prompt registry.
Block prompt releases on material deterministic, rubric, safety, or human-review regressions.
Monitor outcome, refusal, tool, latency, cost, and drift signals without logging sensitive content unnecessarily.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Prompt Registry · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Prompt Registry without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for store owners, versions, model assumptions, templates, and evaluation links in a prompt registry.

content.text: Store owners, versions, model assumptions, templates, and evaluation links in a prompt registry. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Prompt Registry, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a production prompt portfolio and release gate.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Prompt text can be edited in production without traceability. This fails the Prompt Registry contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Anthropic Prompt Engineering Overview (Current 2026-07-13) — Controls success criteria, empirical evaluation, and the living Claude prompt-engineering workflow.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Portfolio Defense trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

content.text: For Portfolio Defense, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

stimulus.title: Portfolio Defense case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A portfolio of successful screenshots proves transferable prompt skill.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend prompt design choices with evidence across Claude, Codex, changed cases, and limitations. Artifact under review: a production prompt portfolio and release gate.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Portfolio Defense, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Portfolio Defense applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend prompt design choices with evidence across Claude, Codex, changed cases, and limitations.

hints: Name the Portfolio Defense precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A portfolio of successful screenshots proves transferable prompt skill.
